exercises.description: A compound supine pressing exercise performed with a narrow (close) grip to place primary emphasis on the triceps while still recruiting the pectorals and anterior delts. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the chest and pressed back to full elbow extension.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Grip the EZ bar on the angled (inner) hand positions so your palms are facing upward/neutral; hands about shoulder-width apart.
Keep your chest up, shoulders back, and core braced to stabilize the spine; elbows should sit close to your sides.
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing your elbows, driving the movement with your biceps; keep your upper arms stationary and forearms moving.
Pause briefly at the top when the bar is near shoulder level and biceps are fully contracted; avoid shrugging or swinging the torso.
Inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control back to full elbow extension without locking the joints.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and strict form throughout.
exercises.description: Standing curl using an EZ bar with a semi-supinated grip to isolate elbow flexion and develop the biceps. Perform controlled concentric and eccentric reps while keeping the torso still to minimize cheating.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown (with rope attachment)
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley with a rope attachment and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a high pulley with a rope attachment and select an appropriate weight.
Stand upright facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight forward lean from the hips; brace your core.
Grasp the rope with a neutral grip so palms face each other and thumbs point inward; elbows should be tucked at your sides and forearms pointing forward with the upper arms nearly vertical.
Inhale, then exhale as you extend your elbows to push the rope downward until your arms are fully straight; at the bottom, separate the rope ends slightly outward to maximize triceps contraction.
Pause briefly at full extension while keeping the elbows stationary and wrists neutral.
Slowly control the return (eccentric) phase by allowing the forearms to rise back to the start position until forearms are approximately parallel to the floor or at about 90° elbow flexion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while avoiding torso sway, shoulder elevation, or elbow travel forward/backward.
If you feel shoulder or lower-back compensation, reduce weight, shorten range slightly, or keep a more upright torso and tighter core brace.
exercises.description: Standing cable rope pushdowns isolate the triceps by extending the elbow against a high-pulley resistance; the rope attachment allows a neutral grip and slight outward flare at full extension to emphasize all triceps heads. Keep the elbows fixed at the sides and move only at the elbow joint for controlled concentric and eccentric phases.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing forward (supinated grip) and arms fully extended.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and fixed in position throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by bending your elbows until the weights reach shoulder level, squeezing the biceps at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining upright posture and avoid leaning back or using momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with full elbow extension under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on strict form and minimizing shoulder or torso movement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the biceps by flexing the elbow against resistance while standing upright; perform controlled reps without swinging to maximize tension on the biceps.

exercises.name: Cable Overhead Triceps Extension Straight Bar
exercises.notes: Step 1: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Step 1: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Step 2: Stand facing away from the machine with feet staggered (one foot slightly forward for balance) and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width, overhand grip.

Step 3: Raise the bar overhead and bend the elbows so the bar is positioned behind your head; keep upper arms close to your ears and elbows pointing upward (slight forward lean at the hips is acceptable).
Step 4: Brace your abs, keep a neutral spine, and stabilize your shoulders (minimize movement of the delts).
Step 5: Exhale and extend both elbows to press the bar upward until your arms are fully straight but not locked aggressively; squeeze the triceps at the top.
Step 6: Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position behind your head with controlled tension (controlled eccentric), keeping the upper arms stationary.
Step 7: Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining strict form; avoid flaring elbows out, arching the lower back, or using momentum from the shoulders.
Step 8: To finish safely, return the bar to the starting support or lower the weight before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: Standing cable overhead triceps extension using a straight bar: perform controlled elbow extensions with the bar starting behind the head and finishing with arms straight overhead to isolate the triceps. Maintain a stable torso and stationary upper arms so the movement occurs at the elbows.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the EZ bar with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width on the angled sections of the bar; wrists should be neutral and aligned with forearms.
instructions: Sit on a preacher (angled) curl bench with feet flat on the floor and chest/abdomen lightly against the pad so your upper arms rest flat on the sloped pad; body should be stable and upright.
Reach forward and grasp the EZ bar with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width on the angled sections of the bar; wrists should be neutral and aligned with forearms.
Start with your arms extended and the bar held just above the pad; this is the start position (elbows slightly bent but not locked).

Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing at the elbows, keeping the upper arms pressed against the pad and avoiding shoulder movement; focus on contracting the biceps as the bar approaches chest level.
Pause and squeeze the biceps briefly at the top of the movement without lifting the elbows off the pad or swinging the torso.
Inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control back to the start position until the arms are near full extension, maintaining contact between upper arms and pad throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, using a weight that allows strict form; avoid using momentum or arching the back to lift the bar.
If wrists or forearms fatigue prematurely, reduce weight or shorten range slightly to maintain strict elbow-driven motion and protect joints.
exercises.description: Seated preacher curl using an EZ bar to isolate the biceps by stabilizing the upper arms on an angled bench pad; emphasizes controlled elbow flexion with minimal shoulder involvement. The angled pad reduces cheating and increases time under tension for the biceps.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter.
instructions: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter. Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight lumbar arch.
Grip: Use a close grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower — roughly 6–12 inches apart) with a pronated grip, wrists stacked over wrists and the bar.
Scapula and position: Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable chest platform; keep shoulders down away from ears.
Unrack: Drive your feet, take a deep breath, brace your core, lock your elbows to lift the bar out of the rack and position it over your sternum with arms vertical.
Descent: Lower the bar in a controlled manner to the lower chest/sternum while keeping your elbows tucked close to your torso (avoid wide flaring), inhaling as you descend.
Touch and pause: Lightly touch or stop just above the chest (avoid bouncing) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Press: Exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows, keeping the bar path slightly diagonal toward the head until near full extension (do not hyperextend or lock aggressively).
Re-rack: After the set, control the bar back to the rack hooks or have a spotter assist. Maintain form on every rep.

Coaching cues & safety: Use a spotter or safety pins for heavy sets, keep wrists neutral, avoid flaring elbows to protect shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain elbow tuck and controlled descent.
exercises.description: A supine barbell press using a narrow grip that shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the lower chest/sternum with elbows kept close to the torso and then pressed back to lockout.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
Hold the barbell with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip so your palms face forward and the bar rests on the base of the fingers.
Start with your arms fully extended down at your thighs, elbows tucked close to your torso and wrists neutral (no excessive bending).
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing the elbows, keeping the upper arms and elbows stationary at your sides — lift until the forearms approach the biceps or the bar is near chest level.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the biceps, avoiding shoulder shrugging or leaning back (no trunk momentum).
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position with a controlled eccentric motion until the arms are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining steady tempo and strict form; reduce weight if you cannot keep elbows stable and avoid swinging the torso.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip; the exercise isolates elbow flexion to work the biceps with the forearms and delts assisting for stabilization. Use a controlled concentric lift and slow eccentric lowering for full muscle stimulation.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back pressed against the pad, feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back pressed against the pad, feet flat on the floor.
Pick up a dumbbell in each hand and let your arms hang straight down toward the floor with palms facing forward (full supination) and shoulders relaxed.
Keep your torso stationary and elbows fixed at your sides/behind your torso; avoid letting them travel forward.
Exhale and curl both dumbbells up by flexing the elbows, squeezing the biceps at the top; rotate the wrists slightly if needed to maintain a full supinated grip.
Hold a brief pause (0.5–1s) at the peak contraction, then inhale and slowly lower the weights back to the fully extended starting position under control.
Perform the movement deliberately without swinging the torso or using momentum; if you cannot control the descent, reduce the weight.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Maintain steady breathing and keep the shoulder blades against the bench to minimize delts compensating.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, dumbbells are curled from a fully extended hanging position to a full biceps contraction, emphasizing the long head by placing the shoulder behind the torso. The incline position increases stretch on the biceps and reduces body momentum.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley and attach a rope (or straight bar).
instructions: Set a high pulley and attach a rope (or straight bar). Select an appropriate weight that allows controlled movement.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and a slight forward lean from the hips; brace your core.
Grasp the rope with a neutral (palms facing) grip and bring your elbows to your sides so your upper arms are vertical and stationary.
Exhale and press the rope down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and the rope ends are slightly spread apart at the bottom.
Pause briefly at full extension while keeping shoulders down and upper arms fixed; avoid using torso swing or stepping for momentum.
Inhale and slowly allow the cable to raise your hands back to the start position, controlling the eccentric phase until your forearms return to vertical.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining tucked elbows and a stable torso throughout the set.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdowns (rope attachment) target the triceps by extending the elbow against resistance while the upper arms remain stationary. The movement also engages the forearms for grip and the delts and abs for posture and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
Keep your torso upright and shoulders back; allow the elbows to rest close to your ribs and remain stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by flexing at the elbow, moving only the forearms so the dumbbells travel toward the front of the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement when the dumbbells reach shoulder level; do not lean back or use momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled extension of the elbows until the arms are straight.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, maintaining a neutral wrist and avoiding swinging the body; if you must swing, reduce the weight.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the elbow flexors by lifting weights from the thighs to shoulder level using a supinated grip. It builds biceps strength and size while the forearms assist in gripping and stabilizing the load.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Lying Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and back pressed into the bench.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and back pressed into the bench.
Grip an EZ bar with a narrow to shoulder-width grip so wrists are comfortable and slightly angled; hold the bar directly over your chest with arms fully extended.
Brace your core and keep your upper arms vertical and stationary (elbows pointing toward the ceiling) throughout the movement.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar by bending only at the elbows, bringing the bar toward your forehead or slightly behind the head (depending on comfort) while maintaining the upper-arm position.
Pause briefly when the forearms approach the target point (forehead/behind head) without letting the elbows flare excessively forward.
Exhale and extend the elbows to press the bar back up to the starting position, fully contracting the triceps but avoiding locking the elbows aggressively.
Maintain controlled tempo, keep wrists neutral on the EZ bar, avoid excessive shoulder movement or arching the lower back, and repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Supine (lying) skull-crusher variation using an EZ curl bar to isolate the triceps by extending the elbows from a flexed position toward full extension. Performed on a flat bench with the upper arms kept mostly stationary to maximize triceps tension.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the EZ bar with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width on the angled sections of the bar; wrists should be neutral and aligned with forearms.
instructions: Sit on a preacher (angled) curl bench with feet flat on the floor and chest/abdomen lightly against the pad so your upper arms rest flat on the sloped pad; body should be stable and upright.
Reach forward and grasp the EZ bar with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width on the angled sections of the bar; wrists should be neutral and aligned with forearms.
Start with your arms extended and the bar held just above the pad; this is the start position (elbows slightly bent but not locked).

Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing at the elbows, keeping the upper arms pressed against the pad and avoiding shoulder movement; focus on contracting the biceps as the bar approaches chest level.
Pause and squeeze the biceps briefly at the top of the movement without lifting the elbows off the pad or swinging the torso.
Inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control back to the start position until the arms are near full extension, maintaining contact between upper arms and pad throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, using a weight that allows strict form; avoid using momentum or arching the back to lift the bar.
If wrists or forearms fatigue prematurely, reduce weight or shorten range slightly to maintain strict elbow-driven motion and protect joints.
exercises.description: Seated preacher curl using an EZ bar to isolate the biceps by stabilizing the upper arms on an angled bench pad; emphasizes controlled elbow flexion with minimal shoulder involvement. The angled pad reduces cheating and increases time under tension for the biceps.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Triceps Pushdown
exercises.notes: Attach a single handle to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a single handle to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grab the handle with one hand in a neutral grip.
Tuck the working elbow to your side so the upper arm is stationary and bent about 90°; brace your core and adopt a slight forward lean from the hips if needed for balance.
Exhale and press the handle down by extending the elbow until the arm is fully straight and the triceps are contracted; keep the wrist neutral.
Pause briefly at full extension, focusing on squeezing the triceps and avoiding shoulder or upper-arm movement.
Inhale and slowly return the handle to the start position under control until the elbow is about 90°; do not let the upper arm drift away from your side.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch to the other arm. Avoid using body momentum or swinging to move the weight.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable pushdown that isolates the triceps by extending the elbow against the cable's resistance while the upper arm remains stationary at the side. Performed standing with a single handle attached to a high pulley for constant tension.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Sled 45 degrees Leg Press
exercises.notes: Set the sled: load appropriate weight, release safety catches, and sit/supine on the sled bench with your back firmly against the angled pad (45 degrees).
instructions: Set the sled: load appropriate weight, release safety catches, and sit/supine on the sled bench with your back firmly against the angled pad (45 degrees).

Foot placement: place both feet shoulder-width on the footplate around mid-plate; toes slightly pointed forward. Adjust feet higher on the plate to emphasize glutes/hamstrings or lower to emphasize quads.
Grip handles at the sides of the sled to stabilize your torso and brace your core (abs) throughout the movement.
Start position: bend your knees until they are approximately 90 degrees (or slightly less) while keeping your lower back pressed into the pad and feet flat on the plate.
Initiate the concentric phase by driving through your heels and extending your knees to push the plate away until legs are nearly straight; do not lock your knees at full extension.
Control the eccentric phase by slowly bending the knees and lowering the sled back to the start position, maintaining foot contact and spinal contact with the pad.
Breathe: exhale during the push (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric).
Stop safely: re-engage safety catches before dismounting or if you cannot control the return; adjust weight if control is lost or form breaks down.
exercises.description: A 45-degree sled leg press where you push a weighted footplate away while lying on an angled sled; it emphasizes knee extension to load the quadriceps with secondary work from the glutes, hamstrings and calves. Perform controlled concentric pushes and slow eccentric returns without locking the knees.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
instructions: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
Unlatch or release the safety so the weight rests on the shoulders and stand tall with a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (not locked).
Inhale, then exhale as you press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, squeezing the calves at the top.
Hold the top position briefly (1 second) while maintaining an upright torso and stable hips.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves.
Control the descent—avoid bouncing—and repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack or secure the lever when finished.
exercises.description: A bilateral standing calf raise performed on a lever/shoulder machine that isolates the calf muscles through plantarflexion. The exercise raises the heels off a platform, then lowers them below platform level for a full stretch.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Cable Donkey Diagonal Kickback
exercises.notes: Attach an ankle cuff to the low cable pulley and secure it around the working ankle.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to the low cable pulley and secure it around the working ankle.
Stand facing the cable machine and place your hands on the frame or a handle at chest height for support; keep the non-working leg slightly bent and weight-bearing.
Hinge forward slightly at the hips so your torso is angled, brace your abs, and keep your spine neutral (avoid arching or rotating the torso).
Start with the working knee bent approximately 90° and the foot close to the floor; this is the start position.
Exhale and drive the working heel back and up in a diagonal line (backward and slightly outward/upward) by contracting the glute, keeping the knee bent throughout the motion.
Pause and squeeze the glute at the top of the movement while maintaining a neutral pelvis and preventing hip rotation.
Inhale and slowly return the leg back to the start position under control, keeping tension on the cable.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch legs. Keep movements smooth and controlled; avoid using momentum or excessive lumbar extension.
exercises.description: A cable-resisted donkey-style kick performed on a diagonal plane to emphasize the glute muscles (particularly the posterior and lateral fibers) while limiting lumbar involvement. The movement drives the heel/back of the foot up and back in a controlled diagonal arc against cable resistance.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and foot platform so your knees are roughly at 90 degrees when your feet are on the plate and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and foot platform so your knees are roughly at 90 degrees when your feet are on the plate and select an appropriate weight.
Sit with your back fully supported against the pad and both feet placed hip-width on the lower edge of the foot platform (balls of feet and toes on the platform, heels free to move).
Grip the handles or seat sides for stability and ensure the safety latch or stops are set before starting.
Unlatch the machine (if applicable) and begin from a neutral/dorsiflexed position with a slight stretch in the calves.
Exhale and press through the balls of your feet by plantarflexing the ankles to raise the platform until your calves are fully contracted; do not use the knees or hips to drive the movement.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the calves, then inhale and slowly lower the platform under control until you feel a stretch in the calves (avoid dropping quickly or bouncing).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then re-engage the safety latch before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: Seated on a lever calf press machine, this exercise uses controlled ankle plantarflexion to raise the foot platform and load the calf muscles. It emphasizes the gastrocnemius/soleus complex while the torso and knees remain supported.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension (Inward Toes)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
instructions: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
Position the lower pad just above your ankles and place your feet on the footpad with your toes turned inward (feet slightly internally rotated). Grip the side handles or seat for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or the machine's start position). Brace your core and keep your spine neutral; do not arch your lower back.
Exhale and extend your knees, pushing the pad upward until your legs are nearly straight but avoid locking the knees at full extension.
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the quads, then inhale and lower the weight slowly and with control back to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining the inward toe position and steady tempo throughout. Stop if you feel sharp knee pain and ensure the movement is smooth and controlled.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever/machine with the toes turned inward; the movement isolates knee extension to target the quadriceps while the inward toe position slightly alters quad activation. The torso stays seated against the back pad and the lower shins press into the machine pad as you extend and lower the legs under control.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
instructions: Sit on the seated calf raise machine so your knees are bent at about 90 degrees and your back is upright (seated position).
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
Position the padded lever across your thighs just above the knees and load the desired plates on the lever (plate-loaded).
Grasp the handles or machine grips for stability and brace your core.
Begin with your heels lowered until you feel a stretch in the calves (ankle dorsiflexion).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible (ankle plantarflexion), lifting the lever and plates.
Pause and squeeze your calves at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping knees bent and torso stable.
Lower your heels slowly and under control back to the starting stretched position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding bouncing or using hip/knee extension to assist the movement.
exercises.description: Seated, plate-loaded calf raise that isolates the calf musculature by plantarflexing the ankles while the knees are bent; the lever pad rests on the thighs and weight is applied via a plate-loaded lever. Performed from a seated position to emphasize the soleus and overall calf development.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart with the barbell over the mid-foot and toes pointing slightly out.
Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
Grip the bar just outside your knees with a double overhand or mixed grip; set shoulders slightly in front of the bar and keep the bar close to your shins.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, keep a neutral (flat) spine and chest lifted — avoid rounding the back.
Drive through the heels and simultaneously extend hips and knees, keeping the bar close to your body and the torso angle changing minimally at first.
Stand tall at the top by finishing hip extension and squeezing the glutes; do not hyperextend the lower back — shoulders should be back but not excessively retracted.
To descend, hinge at the hips first by pushing them back, then bend the knees once the bar passes them, lowering the bar under control to the floor while maintaining a neutral spine.
Reset your breath and core brace at the bottom for the next repetition; maintain consistent foot placement, bar path, and posture throughout the set.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge strength exercise that lifts a loaded barbell from the floor by extending the hips and knees; it targets the posterior chain with heavy involvement of the hamstrings and glutes while the back and core stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the platform and heels hanging off the back; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and stand tall (upright torso).
instructions: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the platform and heels hanging off the back; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and stand tall (upright torso).
Position feet about hip-width apart and keep a slight, natural bend in the knees (do not lock the knees).
Engage your abs to stabilize the torso and maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise your heels by pressing through the balls of your feet until you are fully on your toes and calves are fully contracted; avoid rocking the torso forward.
Pause for 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the step level until you feel a stretch in the calves (control the descent — do not drop abruptly).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping movements smooth and maintaining balance; if needed, reduce weight or use a rail/wall for light support.
exercises.description: Standing calf raise performed on a raised step while holding dumbbells at the sides to load the lower leg; the movement raises the heels by plantarflexing the ankles to target the gastrocnemius/soleus (calves). Emphasis is on controlled concentric raise and slow eccentric lowering below the step for stretch.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Goblet Lunge
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell vertically at chest level with both hands (goblet hold), elbows tucked in.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell vertically at chest level with both hands (goblet hold), elbows tucked in.
Brace your abs, keep your chest tall and shoulders back throughout the movement.
Step forward with one foot a comfortable stride length and lower your body by bending both knees until the front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor and the back knee is close to the ground.
Keep the front knee tracking over the toes (avoid collapsing inward) and distribute weight through the front heel as you descend.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then press through the front heel to drive back up to the starting upright position.

Perform the next repetition by either stepping forward with the opposite leg (alternating) or returning the front foot to the start and repeating on the same side, breathing in on the descent and out as you push up.
exercises.description: A standing lunge variation performed while holding a dumbbell at the chest (goblet hold); it emphasizes the front thigh (quadriceps) while also engaging the glutes and hamstrings for hip drive and the core for stability.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back flat against the backrest, feet under the roller pad and hands holding the side handles or seat edges for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or slightly less) and toes pointing forward; brace your core to keep the torso still.
Exhale and extend your knees by contracting the quads, pushing the roller pad upward until your legs are nearly straight — do not lock the knees.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the contraction in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight with control back to the starting knee angle, keeping tension in the quads throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Avoid using momentum, excessive trunk lean, or hyperextending the knees.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension on a lever/machine that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against a padded roller. The movement is performed from a seated, back-supported position and focuses on controlled knee extension and return.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).
instructions: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).
Set the lower shin pad so it rests just above your ankles when seated; grip the handles or seat edges for stability.
Sit upright with your chest up, core engaged, and feet under the pad (seated position).
Exhale and extend your knees by pressing the pad forward with your lower shins until your legs are nearly straight; avoid hyperextending or locking the knees.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled motion until your knees return to approximately 90°.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping the torso stable and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension on a lever-style machine isolates and strengthens the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance while seated. The torso stays braced against the backpad and the lower shin pushes the pad forward until the knee is nearly straight.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension (Inward Toes)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
instructions: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
Position the lower pad just above your ankles and place your feet on the footpad with your toes turned inward (feet slightly internally rotated). Grip the side handles or seat for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or the machine's start position). Brace your core and keep your spine neutral; do not arch your lower back.
Exhale and extend your knees, pushing the pad upward until your legs are nearly straight but avoid locking the knees at full extension.
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the quads, then inhale and lower the weight slowly and with control back to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining the inward toe position and steady tempo throughout. Stop if you feel sharp knee pain and ensure the movement is smooth and controlled.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever/machine with the toes turned inward; the movement isolates knee extension to target the quadriceps while the inward toe position slightly alters quad activation. The torso stays seated against the back pad and the lower shins press into the machine pad as you extend and lower the legs under control.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Hip Extension
exercises.notes: Attach an ankle cuff to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine and fasten the cuff around the ankle of the working leg; position your feet hip-width apart.
Hold the machine for balance (hands on a handle or the frame) and take a slight forward lean from the hips with a neutral spine; brace your abs.
Keep the supporting knee slightly bent and the working leg straight or with a small bend at the knee throughout the movement.
Drive the working heel backward and slightly upward by contracting the glute until the leg is roughly in line with the torso or slightly behind — avoid arching the lower back or rotating the hips.
Pause and squeeze the glute 1–2 seconds at the top of the movement.
Slowly return the leg to the start position under control, maintaining tension in the glute and hamstrings.
Complete the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat.
exercises.description: Standing cable hip extension performed with an ankle cuff on a low pulley; extend the working leg backward by contracting the glute to drive hip extension while the torso remains stable. The movement emphasizes the gluteus maximus with supporting action from the hamstrings.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
